text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Higher egg consumption was associated with a null effect or a modest reduced risk of cardiovascular disease in observational studies.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In observational studies, higher egg consumption was associated with a null effect or a modest reduced risk of CVD.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states the association observed in observational studies between higher egg intake and CVD risk.","population":"","intervention":"higher egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Observational studies noted positive associations between higher egg intake and type 2 diabetes incidence and CVD risk, while randomised controlled trials saw no effect on markers of T2D and CVD.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"For type 2 diabetes (T2D) incidence and risk of CVD in people with T2D, there were inconsistencies between observational and RCT data, with the former noting positive associations and the latter seeing no effect of higher egg intake on markers of T2D and CVD.","entailment":"The sentence describes the differing findings of observational studies (positive associations) versus RCTs (no effect) regarding higher egg intake and T2D/CVD markers.","population":"people with type 2 diabetes","intervention":"higher egg intake","comparator":"","outcome":"incidence of type 2 diabetes and risk of cardiovascular disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Eggs have the lowest planetary impact among animal proteins according to sustainability metrics.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Sustainability metrics suggest that eggs have the lowest planetary impact amongst animal proteins.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports that sustainability assessments rank eggs as having the smallest planetary footprint compared with other animal proteins.","population":"","intervention":"eggs","comparator":"other animal proteins","outcome":"planetary impact","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Earlier introduction of eggs into weaning diets is warranted to lower allergy risk.","claimType":"value","quote":"To lower allergy risk, earlier introduction of eggs into weaning diets is warranted.","entailment":"The sentence recommends introducing eggs earlier in weaning as a measure to reduce allergy risk, reflecting a normative stance.","population":"","intervention":"earlier introduction of eggs into weaning diets","comparator":"","outcome":"allergy risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"The balance of evidence indicates that eggs are a nutritious food that provides broad health benefits when consumed at intakes higher than current European consumption.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In conclusion, the balance of evidence points to eggs being a nutritious food suggesting there are broad health benefits from including eggs in the diet at intakes higher than that currently consumed by European populations.","entailment":"The concluding sentence summarizes the overall evidence as supporting the nutritional value and health benefits of higher egg consumption in Europe.","population":"European populations","intervention":"higher egg intake than currently consumed","comparator":"","outcome":"broad health benefits","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Earlier concern that dietary cholesterol from eggs significantly raises plasma cholesterol and heart disease risk has been replaced by the view that saturated fat intake has a greater impact.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Earlier concern that dietary cholesterol from eggs and other foods significantly raises plasma cholesterol levels and impacts heart disease risk has been replaced with the view that saturated fat intake has a greater impact.","entailment":"The sentence describes a shift in scientific perspective from focusing on egg cholesterol to emphasizing saturated fat as the dominant factor for heart disease risk.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"In the last two decades, most health bodies in the UK and US dropped specific advice to limit egg consumption to around three per week.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In the last two decades, specific advice to limit egg consumption to around three per week was dropped by most health bodies in the UK and US, while limitations on dietary cholesterol intake in the UK were dropped in 2009.","entailment":"The quoted passage reports that health authorities in the UK and US have ceased recommending a limit of three eggs per week over the past twenty years.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption limit of three per week","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"last two decades","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The United States retained dietary cholesterol restrictions until 2015.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Although most countries removed dietary cholesterol restrictions from food recommendations earlier, the US retained theirs until 2015.","entailment":"The sentence states that the US continued to have dietary cholesterol limits up to the year 2015, unlike other countries.","population":"","intervention":"dietary cholesterol restrictions","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"until 2015","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Raw eggs may be contaminated with salmonella.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Another debated aspect of egg consumption relates to food-borne disease that raw eggs may be contaminated with salmonella.","entailment":"The sentence directly asserts the possibility that raw eggs can carry salmonella contamination.","population":"","intervention":"consumption of raw eggs","comparator":"","outcome":"salmonella contamination","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Eggs can replace meat and fish as a more sustainable protein source.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Food-based dietary guidelines in many European countries now mention that eggs can replace meat and fish as a more sustainable protein source.","entailment":"The quoted guideline statement indicates that eggs are recommended as a sustainable alternative to meat and fish for protein intake.","population":"","intervention":"eggs replacing meat and fish","comparator":"meat and fish","outcome":"sustainability of protein source","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"}]}
